description_long: This object is a cylindrical orange medicine bottle with a wide, ribbed gray cap, reminiscent of prescription pill containers commonly found in pharmacies. The bottle features a paper label with printed text in Russian, including a prominent red heading and smaller black and green text, though some of the label is worn and partially illegible. The cap appears to be made of plastic and is designed for easy gripping and twisting, likely to be child-resistant. The bottle itself is made of translucent orange plastic, showing some surface wear and minor stains, suggesting it has been handled or exposed to the elements. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, and the label is affixed to one side of the bottle. The object is compact and portable, suitable for carrying small quantities of medicine in an urban environment.
description: A worn orange plastic medicine bottle with a ribbed gray twist cap and a partially legible Russian label.
description_view_0: Front view showing the label with Russian text and the ribbed gray cap.
description_view_1: Side view with the label partially visible and more of the bottle's orange surface exposed.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain orange bottle with no label visible, highlighting the cap's ribbed texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the orange bottle surface and cap, label not visible.
